_doc: Pure-kernel latency witness for fak on Apple M3 Pro. Generated 2026-06-20 by the `go test -bench` and `fak bench` harnesses. This is the committed artifact behind the BENCHMARK-AUTHORITY.md pure-kernel rows and MAC-M3PRO-KERNEL-BENCH-2026-06-20.md. Every ns/op below is the median of count=8 trials with the per-trial min/max shown.

provenance.generated_by: go test -run=^$ -bench=. -benchmem -count=8 (adjudicator, ctxmmu); fak bench --baseline-n 100 (syscall A/B)

repro.syscall_ab: go build -o fak ./cmd/fak && ./fak bench --suite tau2-smoke --baseline-n 100 --out report.json

decide_path._doc: The per-tool-call adjudication decision (syscall path), before dispatch. BenchmarkDecide is the canonical allow-path decide; BenchmarkAdjudicateArgScaling adds an ArgPredicate ladder (unrelated-arg count swept 0..2000).

context_admission_scan._doc: ctxmmu.ScreenBytes: the pure in-memory detection scan (no page-out side-effect, no allocation on the secret path). The explainers' cited '~1,300 ns' is the narrow reading of this layer; it reproduces only for the cheapest (secret-regex) path. The injection scan is ~8x slower because it runs the nested repeats()/injection-marker analysis.

BenchmarkScreenBytesSecret._note: closest to the cited ~1,300 ns figure

context_admission_full_gate._doc: ctxmmu.Admit: the full write-time gate including the page-out side-effect a QUARANTINE verdict performs (blob-store write + in-place stub rewrite) and oversize Transform on the benign path. End-to-end per-result admission cost over the testdata/poison.json payloads.

context_admission_chain._doc: The full registered ResultAdmitter chain (normgate rank-5 + ctxmmu), the fold cmd/ctxbench -chain and the kernel Reap exercise. normgate canonicalization (base64/hex decode, unicode/homoglyph fold, letter-run de-separation, broadened secret vocab) dominates the bare-gate cost ~6x on the benign/allow path.

syscall_subsystem_ab._doc: fak bench (tau2-airline-smoke, mock-offline engine): the in-process adjudication boundary vs a process-per-decide spawned `fak hook` baseline. This is the regression sentinel that proves the decide path is resident (no per-call process boundary), NOT a production-throughput headline. n=100 spawned samples.

related_kernel_driven_reproductions._doc: Fresh Mac reproductions of the committed kernel-driven benches (CPU-only; no GPU). These reproduce the published Mac evidence bit-for-bit and are re-witnessed here, not new claims.

honest_read.decide_path: The syscall decision is sub-microsecond on this machine: 362 ns allow, 560-605 ns with an ArgPredicate ladder. The fak-bench in-process p50 (2,427 ns) is the broader Submit+Reap loop, not just Decide, so the two are consistent.
honest_read.cited_1300ns: The '~1,300 ns' admission figure cited in DISAGGREGATED-AGENT-MEMORY.md and MEMORY-LAYERS-EXPLAINER.md is the narrow reading (the secret-regex ScreenBytes scan measures ~1.8 us here). The general ScreenBytes scan is 4.5-14 us, the full Admit (with page-out) is 3.3-15.8 us, and the full normgate+ctxmmu chain is 29-87 us. The cited figure undersells the composed path but is not fabricated; it names the cheapest layer.
honest_read.what_this_does_not_prove: These are microbenchmark latencies on an idle M3 Pro, not production throughput under load, not model quality, and not a serving-rail number. The page-out side-effect cost reflects the in-process blob backend; an external KV backend would shift it.
